title: Per-team memory namespacing of the lessons store
description: Thread an optional per-team namespace through the lessons memory store (src/state/memory.ts) and the retriever (src/orchestrator/memory/retrieve.ts), and update every caller to derive its namespace from the active team. The default/programming namespace resolves to the existing `.bober/memory/` path (no migration, pre-existing lessons stay visible); a named team's namespace resolves to `.bober/memory/<namespace>/`. After this sprint, two teams' lessons are fully isolated in both directions.

successCriteria.description: The project builds successfully with zero TypeScript errors (npm run build).
TypeScript compilation produces zero type errors in strict mode (npm run typecheck).
All tests pass with zero failures, including new collocated namespace tests and the existing memory tests (npm run test).

memory.ts path helpers and public functions (appendLesson, loadLessonIndex, loadLesson) accept an optional namespace; a named namespace resolves to .bober/memory/<namespace>/ and the default/empty namespace resolves to the existing .bober/memory/ path. A unit test asserts both resolved paths.

Isolation: a unit test appends a lesson under namespace 'teamA', then loadLessonIndex for 'teamA' returns it while loadLessonIndex for the default namespace does NOT; and a lesson appended under the default namespace is NOT returned for 'teamA'.

Back-compat: calling the memory functions with no namespace reads/writes the existing .bober/memory/ path; a unit test writing a fixture lesson into .bober/memory/ (no namespace) is still loaded by the no-namespace loadLessonIndex.
retrieveRelevantLessons accepts and honors the namespace, returning only that namespace's lessons. A unit test seeds two namespaces and asserts retrieval is scoped.

The `bober memory` CLI and the chat memory distill (buildMemoryDistill) derive the namespace from the active team (loadTeam), defaulting to the programming/current path. A unit test exercises the CLI/distill namespace resolution (e.g. default team -> current path).

nonGoals: Do not introduce the semantic_facts SQLite table — that is Phase 3 (only the .bober/memory/ lessons store is namespaced here)
Do not migrate existing lessons into a 'programming/' subdir — the default team keeps the current .bober/memory/ path
Do not change pipeline-shape selection — that is Sprint 3
Do not add CLI --team flags or the example team — that is Sprint 4
Do not add memory hygiene/decay or reconcile-on-write (Phase 3+)
stopConditions: Lessons are isolated per namespace in both directions and the default/programming path is unchanged with pre-existing lessons still visible
definitionOfDone: memory.ts and retrieve.ts accept an optional namespace; memoryDir(projectRoot, namespace?) returns .bober/memory/ for the default/empty namespace and .bober/memory/<namespace>/ otherwise. appendLesson, loadLessonIndex, loadLesson, and retrieveRelevantLessons thread the namespace. All callers (src/cli/commands/memory.ts, src/chat/chat-session.ts buildMemoryDistill, src/orchestrator/memory/distill.ts) derive the namespace from the active team via loadTeam, defaulting to programming (current path). Lessons are fully isolated across namespaces; the default path and pre-existing lessons are untouched. Build, typecheck, and all tests (new + existing memory tests) pass.
assumptions: The programming/default team's memoryNamespace sentinel (from Sprint 1) maps to the existing .bober/memory/ path with no subdir, so no migration is needed.
Namespace values are constrained to safe path segments (alphanumeric/underscore/hyphen) — already enforced by the Sprint 1 schema regex — preventing path traversal.
The INDEX.md file is per-namespace (.bober/memory/<ns>/INDEX.md), so each team has its own bounded index.
generatorNotes:
Thread namespace as an optional trailing parameter to preserve back-compat for any caller not yet updated.

Verified touch points:
- src/state/memory.ts: memoryDir(projectRoot) at :15, lessonPath at :19, indexPath at :23 — add optional namespace and, when present+non-empty, join MEMORY_DIR/<namespace>. appendLesson at :195, loadLessonIndex at :241, loadLesson — add optional namespace and pass through to the path helpers. Keep INDEX_FILE per-namespace.
- src/orchestrator/memory/retrieve.ts: retrieveRelevantLessons(projectRoot, keywords, opts) at :79 — add namespace to opts (or a param) and pass through to loadLessonIndex/loadLesson.
- Callers to update to derive namespace from the active team (loadTeam(config, teamId).memoryNamespace, default programming -> '' -> current path):
  - src/cli/commands/memory.ts: appendLesson:68, loadLessonIndex:58 & :93, loadLesson:139. Add an optional --team (or --namespace) option that resolves via loadTeam; default keeps current path.
  - src/chat/chat-session.ts: buildMemoryDistill at :41 (loadLessonIndex:43, loadLesson:49). The session already knows its config/team context; pass the active team's namespace.
  - src/orchestrator/memory/distill.ts: appendLesson — write lessons under the active run's team namespace (default programming).

Back-compat rule: namespace undefined OR '' OR 'programming' -> .bober/memory/ (current path, no subdir). Any other value -> .bober/memory/<value>/. Centralize this mapping in one helper so every path computation agrees.

Use node:fs/promises (async) and ensureDir; no fs mocks — use real temp dirs in tests and clean up (project principle). Reuse LessonEntrySchema/serialization unchanged.

Tests: collocate in src/state/memory.test.ts (extend) and a new namespace test. Seed two namespaces in a temp projectRoot, assert isolation both directions, assert default path round-trips a pre-existing fixture, assert retrieveRelevantLessons is scoped.
evaluatorNotes:
sc-2-4: assert memoryDir(root) === <root>/.bober/memory and memoryDir(root,'teamA') === <root>/.bober/memory/teamA; same for lessonPath/indexPath.
sc-2-5: appendLesson(root, lessonA, 'teamA'); loadLessonIndex(root,{...},'teamA') includes lessonA; loadLessonIndex(root,{...}) (default) excludes it. Then appendLesson(root, lessonB) (default); loadLessonIndex(root,{...},'teamA') excludes lessonB.
sc-2-6: manually write a valid lesson .md + INDEX line into <root>/.bober/memory/ and assert no-namespace loadLessonIndex returns it (proves default path unchanged).
sc-2-7: seed lessons in 'teamA' and 'teamB'; retrieveRelevantLessons(root, keywords, {namespace:'teamA'}) returns only teamA's.
sc-2-8: invoke the memory CLI / buildMemoryDistill with the default team and assert it reads the current path; with a named team assert it reads the subdir.
Run npm run build, npm run typecheck, npm run test (must include existing memory tests green).
